title: Watch how coffee flavor changes.
title2: Move the grind and the ratio.
title3: Advanced Mode for Temperature, Pressure, and Water.
subtitle: An educational heuristic based on extraction theory. Useful to understand whether your recipe will lean more acidic, more bitter, or fuller-bodied.
adjust: Adjust the recipe and type of coffee. The result is updated in real time.
state_desc_sub: You’ll likely get more pronounced acidity and a lighter body. Try grinding finer or slightly increasing the ratio.
state_desc_bal: Sweet spot: more sweetness and clarity in the cup.
state_desc_over: Bitterness or astringency may show up. Try grinding coarser or shortening the ratio.
note: Note: This is an educational heuristic, not a physical measurement.
style_ristretto_desc: Shorter and more concentrated: more intensity and body, less clarity.
style_espresso_desc: Classic balance: a good mix of intensity, sweetness and clarity.
style_lungo_desc: Longer: often more clarity, but it increases the risk of bitterness and astringency.
roastAdvice: Tip: light roasts tend to punish underextraction more, while dark roasts bring bitterness earlier.
controlsDescription: Adjust the recipe and coffee type. The result updates in real time.
mapHelpP1: The horizontal axis represents ratio and the vertical axis represents grind. The dot shows where your recipe lands.
mapHelpP2: Blue indicates underextraction tendency, green the balanced zone, and red overextraction tendency.
mapHelpP3: Finer grind usually moves the recipe toward higher extraction. Coarser grind toward lower extraction. A longer ratio shifts the point to the right.
mapHelpP4: Temperature and pressure act as secondary adjustments and slightly shift the point.
radarHelpP1: The radar summarizes the estimated sensory profile of the recipe across five axes: acidity, sweetness, bitterness, astringency, and body.
radarHelpP2: A higher value does not necessarily mean better: it indicates a stronger presence of that attribute in the cup.
radarHelpP3: Underextracted recipes usually show more acidity and less body. Overextracted ones tend to increase bitterness and astringency.
radarHelpP4: Roast, process, temperature, pressure, and water modify the final profile in addition to grind and ratio.
noSavedRecipes: You haven't saved any recipes yet.
selectToCompare: Select two recipes to compare them.
engineExplain: How does the simulation model work?
engineInfo1: Coffee-sim uses a heuristic extraction model designed to approximate how espresso brewing variables interact. The engine transforms recipe parameters into a normalized space and computes an estimated extraction index between 0 and 100.
engineInfo2: The primary drivers of the model are grind size and brew ratio. Grind acts as a modifier of contact surface and hydraulic resistance of the coffee bed, while ratio controls the relative amount of dissolved solids extracted. Together they define the main extraction axis.
engineInfo3: On top of this base axis, coffee-dependent modulators are applied. Roast level introduces structural biases in extraction ease: light roasts penalize underextraction and emphasize acidity, while darker roasts increase the tendency toward bitterness and body.
engineInfo4: Coffee processing method acts as an additional sensory modulator. Washed coffees increase clarity and perceived acidity, natural processes reinforce sweetness and body, and honey processes sit between both profiles. These effects are applied as weighted adjustments on the final sensory axes.
engineInfo5: Advanced parameters alter the system balance. Temperature changes thermal extraction efficiency and shifts the extraction toward more bitter or more acidic profiles. Pressure modifies espresso flow dynamics, indirectly affecting extraction yield.
engineInfo6: From the estimated extraction value, the engine projects the result onto five sensory axes: acidity, sweetness, bitterness, astringency and body. These axes are computed using non-linear response curves modeling typical coffee extraction behavior.
engineInfo7: The final output is a probabilistic sensory representation. The model does not attempt to reproduce the full physics of espresso, but instead captures the dominant relationships between brewing variables and cup perception.
engineInfo8: Model weights were calibrated using extraction theory, barista experience and iterative tuning assisted by artificial intelligence.
state_desc_sub_espresso: Likely pronounced acidity and light body. Try grinding finer or shortening the ratio.
state_desc_bal_espresso: Sweet spot for espresso: sweetness and clarity with good intensity.
state_desc_over_espresso: Possible bitterness or astringency. Try grinding coarser or slightly shortening the ratio.
state_desc_sub_v60: The cup may taste thin and acidic. Grind finer or increase brew time.
state_desc_bal_v60: Balanced extraction: floral sweetness, clarity and clean body typical of V60.
state_desc_over_v60: Possible bitterness or astringency. Grind coarser or reduce total time.
state_desc_sub_french_press: Likely acidity and weak body. Grind finer or steep longer.
state_desc_bal_french_press: Good zone: rich and smooth body without excessive bitterness or acidity.
state_desc_over_french_press: Possible elevated bitterness and astringency. Grind coarser or reduce steep time.
state_desc_sub_aeropress: The cup may be acidic and light. Grind finer, increase temperature or brew longer.
state_desc_bal_aeropress: Balanced extraction: clarity, sweetness and good body typical of Aeropress.
state_desc_over_aeropress: Possible bitterness or astringency. Grind coarser or reduce time and pressure.
state_desc_sub_moka: The moka pot may taste watery or acidic. Increase heat or use a finer grind.
state_desc_bal_moka: Good zone for moka: intense body and sweetness with balanced bitterness.
state_desc_over_moka: Possible burnt or strong bitterness. Lower heat or use a coarser grind.
state_desc_sub_cold_brew: The cold brew may be acidic and light. Steep longer or use a finer grind.
state_desc_bal_cold_brew: Balanced extraction: gentle sweetness, low acidity and silky body of cold brew.
state_desc_over_cold_brew: Bitterness or astringency may appear. Reduce steep time or use a coarser grind.
